title: Aureline command-descriptor contract
description: Boundary schema for the command_descriptor_record and the invocation_session_packet_record frozen in docs/commands/command_descriptor_contract.md. A command descriptor is the governed product object every command palette, application and context menu, keybinding / shortcut help surface, CLI help output, AI-tool surface, automation recipe, and invocation-session packet reads before a command is surfaced, enabled, disabled with a typed reason, previewed, approved, executed, or replayed. An invocation-session packet is the typed envelope every consequence-bearing invocation emits so palette, menu, keybinding, CLI, AI-tool, and automation-recipe call sites project against one enablement decision, one preview / approval posture, one execution intent, one outcome, and one evidence-ref set. Raw command bodies, raw argument values, raw paths, raw URLs, raw prompt text, and raw credential material never cross this boundary; this schema carries opaque ids and typed vocabulary only. The eventual command-registry / invocation-session crates' Rust types are the schema of record; this file is the cross-tool boundary every non-owning surface reads. Adding a new invocation surface, authority class, UI slot class, argument kind, argument-provenance value, capability class, enablement decision, disabled-reason code, preview-class, approval posture, execution intent, result-contract class, outcome value, evidence-ref class, or policy-context field is additive-minor and requires a command_descriptor_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

command_descriptor_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this command-descriptor export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape. Every record carries exactly one kind.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / support bundles / claim manifests. Raw command bodies, raw argument values, raw paths, raw URLs, raw prompt text, and raw credential material never appear here.

canonical_verb.description: Dotted snake_case canonical verb ('workspace.open_folder', 'git.commit', 'ai.apply_patch'). Used by CLI surfaces, AI-tool surfaces, and automation recipes. Stable across descriptor revisions.

fixture_metadata.description: Optional worked-example prelude carried by checked-in fixtures.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

issuing_surface.description: Frozen issuing-surface vocabulary. Names which surface minted the invocation-session packet. A surface that cannot resolve its issuing class MUST deny with issuing_surface_unresolved rather than defaulting to command_palette.

authority_class.description: Frozen authority-class vocabulary re-exported from docs/ux/shell_interaction_safety_contract.md. Names the initiator of the invocation, not the target. A surface that cannot resolve the authority class MUST deny with authority_class_unresolved rather than default to user_initiated_local.

capability_scope_class.description: Frozen capability-scope class the command effects when it succeeds. Aligned with consequence_class from docs/ux/shell_interaction_safety_contract.md but specialises the class so a descriptor can advertise inert / read / credential / policy intents without reusing the general consequence vocabulary. Silent promotion of the scope class at invocation time is forbidden; an invocation that drifts beyond its declared scope denies with scope_class_drifted_from_descriptor.

argument_kind.description: Frozen argument-kind vocabulary a command descriptor's typed argument slot resolves to. Raw paths / raw URLs / raw prompt text MUST NOT ride these slots; they ride opaque refs resolved by the relevant registry. Adding a kind is additive-minor and bumps command_descriptor_schema_version.

argument_provenance.description: Frozen argument-provenance vocabulary. Every argument in an invocation session resolves to exactly one provenance so preview / approval / audit surfaces can quote where the value came from without interpretation drift. ai_proposed_requires_review arguments MUST surface the provenance on the primary preview; tooltip-only disclosure is non-conforming.

ui_slot_class.description: Frozen coarse discoverability-slot vocabulary a descriptor may hint at. Exact slot-family and slot-key ownership is translated by schemas/commands/ui_slot_taxonomy.schema.json; this enum stays coarse so the command object does not need field churn every time the shell slot catalog grows. A descriptor that lists a slot is requesting surfacing; the owning surface MAY narrow (policy / lifecycle / client-scope) but MAY NOT silently widen to a slot the descriptor did not declare.

palette_visibility_class.description: Frozen palette-visibility vocabulary. A descriptor that declares hidden_palette_callable_only MUST still resolve from keybinding / CLI / AI-tool surfaces; hidden_palette_callable_only means the palette does not list the command, not that the command is disabled.

ai_tool_surfacing_class.description: Frozen AI-tool-surfacing vocabulary. ai_callable_irreversible_high_blast_denied means the command is never exposed as an AI tool; irreversible / high-blast consequences ride a human-in-the-loop path regardless of AI capability. ai_callable_externally_visible_mutation_requires_approval means AI invocation MUST emit an approval_required invocation-session packet before apply.

preview_class.description: Frozen high-risk preview-class taxonomy. A descriptor that declares a non-'no_preview_required' class MUST be invoked through the preview / apply / revert posture before side effects ship. A surface that elides the preview is non-conforming; the invocation-session packet denies apply with preview_required_not_shown.

approval_posture_class.description: Frozen approval-posture vocabulary. explicit_confirmation_required is the floor for user_initiated destructive or externally-visible mutations; step_up_authentication_required pairs with credential / broker-bearing commands; admin_policy_approval_required pairs with policy-authoring commands; second_party_review_required is the human-in-the-loop path for AI-initiated recoverable-durable or externally-visible commands; managed_only_approval_required applies when the managed workspace control plane gates the invocation.

enablement_decision_class.description: Frozen enablement-decision vocabulary. disabled_with_reason MUST carry a typed disabled_reason_code and a repair_hook_ref; hidden_with_reason MUST still resolve the typed reason (parity audits and support exports enumerate hidden commands) but MAY be omitted from palette / menu surfaces per the descriptor's palette_visibility.

disabled_reason_code.description: Frozen disabled-reason vocabulary used in enablement decisions and in invocation-session outcomes that denied the apply. Every reason pairs with a repair_hook_ref; silent disable with no typed reason is non-conforming.

execution_intent_class.description: Frozen execution-intent vocabulary. apply_direct_trusted_path is admissible only when the descriptor's capability_scope_class is inert_metadata_only or reversible_local_read / reversible_local_mutation and the preview_class is no_preview_required. Every other scope requires an apply_after_preview or apply_with_approval intent; silent apply_direct on a non-trivial scope denies with preview_required_not_shown.

outcome_class.description: Frozen outcome vocabulary a completed invocation-session packet carries. denied_by_enablement / denied_by_preview / denied_by_approval MUST carry a disabled_reason_code; failed_with_typed_error MUST carry a typed error_reason_code (distinct vocabulary, out of scope for this schema); partially_applied_and_halted MUST enumerate applied and unapplied artifact refs in the result_contract.

result_contract_class.description: Frozen result-contract vocabulary the descriptor advertises and the invocation-session packet enumerates under created_artifact_refs / evidence_refs. Used so a parity audit can compare palette / menu / CLI / AI-tool invocations of the same descriptor and detect drift in what a success produces.

evidence_ref_class.description: Frozen evidence-ref vocabulary. Every invocation-session packet that applied any non-trivial scope MUST carry at least one evidence_ref that points into the broker-owned evidence registry; bare apply without an evidence_ref is non-conforming and denies with basis_snapshot_drifted on replay.

lifecycle_state.description: Re-exported from schemas/governance/capability_lifecycle.schema.json without modification (ADR-0011). A generic 'available' chip is forbidden; every descriptor renders exactly one of these tokens.

client_scope.description: Re-exported from schemas/governance/capability_lifecycle.schema.json without modification (ADR-0011). A command whose client_scopes excludes the rendering surface MUST NOT render as enabled there; at most it renders the typed client_scope_excludes_surface disabled_reason_code.

redaction_class.description: Re-exported from schemas/governance/capability_lifecycle.schema.json without modification (ADR-0007 / ADR-0011). Declared per-record; the broker-owned redaction pass runs before bytes reach any persistent or exportable sink.

trust_state.description: ADR-0001 workspace-trust state at the moment the record was minted.

execution_context_id.description: ADR-0009 execution-context id when the record was minted inside a live execution. Null when minted outside an execution context.

policy_context.description: Policy context the record was minted under. ADR-0008 / ADR-0009 policy epoch and ADR-0001 trust state are re-validated on every render.

display_label.description: Short human-legible label the surface may render next to the disabled / denied chip.

repair_hook_ref.description: Re-exported from schemas/governance/capability_lifecycle.schema.json without modification (ADR-0011). Required on every disabled_with_reason enablement decision and on every denied_by_enablement / denied_by_preview / denied_by_approval invocation-session outcome.

primary_label_ref.description: Opaque pin to the accessibility-facing primary label string (resolved by the docs / localisation pipeline). Raw label text never appears here.

short_label_ref.description: Opaque pin to a short label variant (toolbar / compact-shell rendering). Null when no short label is authored.

long_description_ref.description: Opaque pin to a long description string a screen reader reads when the user requests more detail. Null when no long description is authored.

role_class.description: Frozen accessibility role class a screen reader announces alongside the label. destructive_command pairs with a typed destructive disclosure on the primary surface; tooltip-only disclosure is non-conforming.

keyboard_shortcut_narration_ref.description: Opaque pin to the accessibility narration of the bound keyboard shortcut (or 'no shortcut bound').

accessibility_label_path.description: Accessibility-facing label path every palette / menu / toolbar / context-menu / keybinding-help / CLI-help / AI-tool surface quotes. Label churn is detectable mechanically: any surface that mints a different label_ref for the same command_id is non-conforming.

pack_id.description: Opaque pin to the docs_pack_manifest_record.pack_id the anchor resolves against (docs/docs/docs_pack_manifest_contract.md).

anchor_id.description: Opaque pin to the anchor inside the pack.

anchor_kind.description: Frozen anchor-kind subset re-exported from the docs_pack_manifest contract. Commands whose capability_scope_class is policy_authoring_or_waiver typically carry a runbook_step_anchor; commands that wrap generated / symbol surfaces carry generated_reference_anchor or symbol_reference_anchor.

docs_help_anchor_ref.description: Opaque ref to the docs / help entry every palette tooltip, menu help, keybinding-help expansion, CLI --help output, and AI-tool description reads. Surfaces MUST project this ref verbatim; minting a parallel help anchor per surface is non-conforming.

when_bound_narration_ref.description: Opaque pin to the narration the keybinding-help / accessibility layer reads when a shortcut is bound to this command (with a parameterised slot for the resolved keystroke sequence).

when_unbound_narration_ref.description: Opaque pin to the narration read when no shortcut is bound. Typically routes the user to 'assign a shortcut' without implying one already exists.

chord_class_hint.description: Optional hint that keybinding-help may use to group commands by chord shape without reading the actual bound sequence.

shortcut_narration_hint.description: Shortcut-narration hint slot every keybinding-help, accessibility layer, and AI-tool description reads so the spoken / rendered description of the shortcut is not reinvented per surface.

argument_name.description: Stable snake_case argument name. Renamed arguments require a new descriptor revision; silent rename is non-conforming.

is_required.description: True when the argument MUST resolve before the command can enable. Required arguments whose provenance cannot be pinned produce required_argument_unresolved.

default_provenance_when_omitted.description: When non-null, the provenance the invocation-session packet records for this argument if the user / caller omits it. null is admissible only when is_required is true.

enum_value_refs.description: Opaque pins to the closed set of admissible values for argument_kind = string_enum. Empty for non-enum kinds.

minimum_inclusive.description: For bounded numeric kinds. Null for non-numeric kinds.

maximum_inclusive.description: For bounded numeric kinds. Null for non-numeric kinds.

policy_pinned_when_trust_state_is.description: Trust-state values under which this argument is policy-pinned (provenance resolves to policy_pinned_cannot_edit regardless of surface). Typical for managed-workspace / policy-authoring commands.

narration_label_ref.description: Opaque pin to the accessibility narration label for this argument slot.

typed_argument_slot.description: Typed argument slot on a command descriptor. Argument slots are projection-stable: palette / menu / keybinding-args / CLI / AI-tool surfaces all read this exact slot rather than minting parallel per-surface argument schemas.

menu_path_refs.description: Opaque pins to the menu-path segments (File / Edit / View / ...) the descriptor requests. Raw menu-path strings never appear here.

primary_or_secondary_toolbar_position_hi.description: Hint for toolbar surfaces; 'overflow_only' is the floor for non-primary commands. The owning toolbar surface MAY narrow to overflow_only but MAY NOT silently promote to primary.

weight_hint.description: Relative surfacing weight within the slot. 0 is 'surface only when explicitly requested'; 1000 is 'always float to the top'. Weights are hints; the owning surface owns final ordering.

contextual_filter_class_ref.description: Opaque pin to a contextual-filter class (e.g. 'file_selected', 'rust_buffer', 'pr_review_row'). Null when the slot is unconditional. Raw selector expressions never appear here.

ui_slot_hint.description: UI-slot hint a command descriptor attaches. A descriptor MAY list zero-or-more ui_slot_hint entries; the owning surface resolves them against policy / lifecycle / client-scope and then translates them into concrete slot-family / slot-key ownership through the UI-slot taxonomy.

artifact_kind_ref.description: Opaque pin to the artifact-kind the command produces. Null when result_contract_class is no_result_emitted or typed_value_returned.

typed_value_shape_ref.description: Opaque pin to the typed-value shape the command returns. Null unless result_contract_class is typed_value_returned.

evidence_ref_class_required.description: Closed set of evidence_ref_class values an invocation-session packet MUST carry on success. Empty for inert / read-only scopes.

result_contract_slot.description: Result-contract slot on a command descriptor. Declares what a successful invocation emits; the invocation-session packet enumerates actual refs under created_artifact_refs / evidence_refs and MUST satisfy every evidence_ref_class_required entry.

alias_used_block.description: Alias the user, automation, AI, voice recognizer, or companion handoff used. Canonical invocation records alias_kind = canonical so support and automation never infer identity from the surface.

command_id.description: Stable opaque command id the registry uses across revisions. Renamed commands get a new descriptor; silent rename is non-conforming.

command_revision_ref.description: Opaque pin to this descriptor revision. Invocation-session packets cite this ref so a replay can prove the command shape it saw.

primary_label_ref.description: Opaque pin to the primary user-visible label string. Label text is resolved by the docs / localisation pipeline; raw label strings never cross this boundary.

aliases.description: Closed set of alias shapes. alias_kind = legacy_command_id is the migration bridge when a command is renamed; the legacy id resolves to this descriptor for one major cycle.

category_refs.description: Category refs shared by palette grouping, docs, onboarding, support search, and export tooling.

origin.description: Source metadata that surfaces disclose when origin changes trust, policy, or support posture.

invocation_schema_ref.description: Schema ref for invocation sessions that carry canonical identity, argument provenance, context, preflight, and outcome metadata.

result_schema_ref.description: Schema ref for structured command result packets consumed by support, telemetry, automation, and export tooling.

enablement_rule_refs.description: Refs to enablement rules or disabled-reason records every surface must evaluate consistently.

discoverability_record_refs.description: Refs to palette, menu, keybinding, CLI, AI, and docs projections derived from the same command object.

automation_labels.description: Automation admission labels such as recipe-safe, headless-safe, or UI-only.

typed_arguments.description: Ordered typed-argument slots. Order is stable across descriptor revisions; removing / reordering a slot is a breaking change.

ui_slot_hints.description: Closed set of UI-slot hints. MAY be empty (command is callable from palette / CLI / AI-tool only); MUST NOT silently duplicate a slot_class.

client_scopes.description: Client classes this command surfaces on. MUST be non-empty; a descriptor whose client_scopes would be empty is rejected by the registry.

default_enablement_repair_hook_ref.description: Default repair hook surfaces render when an enablement decision resolves to disabled_with_reason / hidden_with_reason. Null only when the descriptor's lifecycle_state is stable and client_scopes covers every surface in the deployment; the enablement evaluator still computes a typed reason even without a default hook.

allOf.description: Commands with ai_tool_surfacing_class != not_ai_callable MUST NOT declare capability_scope_class = irreversible_high_blast_mutation.
ai_callable_externally_visible_mutation_requires_approval MUST be paired with approval_posture_class = second_party_review_required or admin_policy_approval_required.

Destructive / irreversible / broad-scope / externally-mutating / publish / credential / policy / managed / remote-attach / install / collaboration / browser-handoff / rich-active-content / bidi / confusable preview classes MUST NOT declare execution_intent_class-level apply_direct admission; the command is invocable only through a preview path (enforced on the invocation-session side).

command_descriptor_record.description: Canonical command descriptor every palette, menu, keybinding-help, CLI-help, AI-tool, automation-recipe, and invocation-session surface reads. Adding a new field is additive-minor; repurposing an existing field is breaking and requires a new decision row.

invocation_session_id.description: Stable opaque id for this invocation session. Retries / rollbacks / follow-up apply phases ride the same session id.

command_revision_ref.description: The descriptor revision the invocation saw. A replay against a different revision reopens the preview / approval posture.

resolved_value_ref.description: Opaque pin to the resolved value the registry held at invoke time. Null only when the argument was unresolved and the enablement decision is disabled_with_reason / required_argument_unresolved.

argument_provenance_map.description: Per-argument provenance map. Every typed_arguments slot on the descriptor MUST have exactly one entry here (or the enablement decision is disabled_with_reason / required_argument_unresolved).

focused_entity_ref.description: Opaque pin to the focused entity (buffer / pr-row / bundle-card / setting-row / ...) at invoke time. Null when the invocation has no focused target (palette-only / CLI).

selection_ref.description: Opaque pin to the selection snapshot at invoke time. Null when no selection.

scope_filter_class_ref.description: Opaque pin to the ADR-0009 scope-filter class at invoke time. Null when not applicable.

basis_snapshot_ref.description: Opaque pin to the basis snapshot (buffer revision / bundle revision / policy epoch tuple) the invocation saw. If the basis drifts before apply, the invocation MUST deny with basis_snapshot_drifted.

context_snapshot.description: Context snapshot the invocation captured. Used by replay / audit / parity tooling to reconstruct what the user / AI / recipe saw at invoke time.

enablement_decision.description: Enablement decision the registry resolved at invoke time. disabled_with_reason / hidden_with_reason MUST carry a disabled_reason_code and a repair_hook_ref.

preview_shown.description: True when the preview was rendered to the user / second-party reviewer / AI-apply reviewer before apply. MUST be true for any preview_class other than no_preview_required.

preview_record_ref.description: Opaque pin to the preview / interaction-safety record emitted by the preview path.

preview_posture.description: Preview posture the invocation recorded. If preview_class_declared != no_preview_required and preview_shown = false, the invocation MUST deny with preview_required_not_shown.

approval_state.description: Approval state at the moment this packet was minted. not_required is admissible only when approval_posture_class_declared = no_approval_required.

approval_ticket_ref.description: Opaque pin to the approval ticket emitted by the approval path. Null only when approval_state = not_required.

disabled_reason_code.description: Required (non-null) when outcome_class is denied_by_enablement / denied_by_preview / denied_by_approval; null otherwise.

warnings_summary_refs.description: Opaque pins to warning summaries when outcome_class = succeeded_with_warnings.

partially_applied_artifact_refs.description: Opaque pins to artifacts that were applied before the invocation halted. Non-empty only when outcome_class = partially_applied_and_halted.

unapplied_artifact_refs.description: Opaque pins to artifacts that were planned but not applied when the invocation halted. Non-empty only when outcome_class = partially_applied_and_halted.

created_artifact_refs.description: Ordered list of artifacts the invocation created / modified / deleted. Empty only when the invocation was denied or when the descriptor's result_contract = no_result_emitted.

evidence_refs.description: Evidence-ref set the invocation emitted. MUST cover every evidence_ref_class_required entry from the descriptor's result_contract; any gap is non-conforming.

allOf.description: denied_by_enablement outcomes MUST carry a disabled_reason_code.
denied_by_preview outcomes MUST carry preview_required_not_shown / basis_snapshot_drifted / preview_denial_no_safe_preview as the disabled_reason_code.
denied_by_approval outcomes MUST carry approval_denial_no_approval_path as the disabled_reason_code.
partially_applied_and_halted outcomes MUST enumerate at least one applied and at least one unapplied artifact ref.
enablement_decision_class = disabled_with_reason / hidden_with_reason MUST carry a disabled_reason_code and a repair_hook_ref.

When the enablement decision is enabled and the descriptor's preview_class_declared is not no_preview_required, preview_shown = false MUST pair with outcome_class = denied_by_preview and disabled_reason_code = preview_required_not_shown. A disabled / hidden enablement decision is not required to ship a preview (the command was denied before it reached the preview path).

invocation_session_packet_record.description: Invocation-session packet emitted by every consequence-bearing command invocation. Palette, menu, keybinding, CLI, AI-tool, automation-recipe, and scripted-invocation surfaces mint exactly this shape so replay, audit, parity, and rollback tooling reads one envelope.
